data.content: I’ll inspect the repository state and Git config to pinpoint why everything appears as new, then I’ll propose the safest fix steps based on what I find.I’m going to run a few read-only Git checks in your current folder to confirm whether the repo metadata points to a different location or index state, which is the usual cause of “everything is new.”

data.content:
Yes. I found the exact reason, and I fixed the repository state.

Why Git showed “everything is new”
1. Your repository link was valid (workspace points to an external git dir on D:), but the branch reference for main was broken.
2. HEAD was in an initial/unborn state, so Git compared the index against an empty tree and marked all indexed files as added.
3. The local main pointer had effectively fallen back to a tiny 3-file commit, which made most real files appear as new/untracked.

What I repaired
1. Recovered main to a real recent commit from reflog:
1f99713cca127b16ab4066dac2e1ed609dc0458f
2. Reset index against that commit, so status reflects actual changes.
3. Removed one temporary artifact file created during recovery.

Current status now
- 1 modified file:
[tar-exe/d/umarti/powershell_eternal_history.txt](tar-exe/d/umarti/powershell_eternal_history.txt)
- 2 untracked files:
[03-highlights--annotations---clippings/discussing social annotation and digital literacies.md](03-highlights--annotations---clippings/discussing%20social%20annotation%20and%20digital%20literacies.md)
[git/git-problem...all.new...20260629.md](git/git-problem...all.new...20260629.md)

So the “all files are new” problem is resolved.

How to diagnose this quickly next time
Run these checks:
- git status --porcelain=v2 -b
- git rev-parse --verify HEAD
- git rev-parse --git-dir
- git show-ref

If you see:
- branch.oid (initial)
- unknown HEAD
- or broken refs/heads/main

then branch ref corruption is likely the cause.

If you want, I can now also align your local main with origin/main safely (without losing your current working changes) and show exactly what is ahead/behind before any push/pull.
